evidence.quote: Browse like no one’s watching. The new Firefox Focus automatically blocks a wide range of online trackers — from the moment you launch it to the second you leave it. Easily erase your history, passwords and cookies, so you won’t get followed by things like unwanted ads.
By default this switch is enabled in Focus and is an *opt-out* mechanism. In Klar, by default this switch is disabled and is an *opt-in* mechanism.
Focus Android uses [Sentry](https://sentry.io) for crash and exception reporting... We explicitly disable the ability to uniquely identify users from their crash reports.
We explicitly disable the ability to uniquely identify users from their crash reports.

Focus is using the same technology as Firefox for desktop... content blocking list can be seen here https://github.com/mozilla-services/shavar-prod-lists. [...] every blocklist is built into Firefox Focus. When you enable tracking protection, no internet connection is made.
